title: Multimodal Cognitive Workload n-back Task, 4 Difficulties
description: This multimodal dataset comprises synchronized EEG, electrocardiography (ECG), and eye-tracking data collected during an n-back working memory task with four difficulty levels. Eighteen participants (2 of 20 recorded participants excluded due to data quality failures) completed a tutorial phase followed by a main experiment involving letter-matching trials at 1-back through 4-back levels. The dataset includes raw physiological recordings acquired via a mobile EEG system and Pupil Labs eye tracker, with comprehensive event annotations and participant metadata.
methods_description: Data were collected using a combined mobile EEG+ECG system (19-channel 10-20 montage, 250 Hz sampling rate, Ag/AgCl electrodes with linked-ear reference) and Pupil Labs Pupil Core eye tracker, synchronized via Lab Streaming Layer (LSL). The n-back task protocol included a tutorial phase (20 trials per difficulty level with feedback) and main experiment (100 trials per level without feedback), with each trial presenting a letter for 1.0 s followed by a 0.7 s blank interval. Task events and acquisition markers were extracted from LSL streams and aligned to EEG start times.
